name: Ellis Larkins
abstract: Ellis Larkins (May 15, 1923 – September 29, 2002) was an American jazz pianist born in Baltimore, Maryland, known for his two recordings with Ella Fitzgerald: the albums Ella Sings Gershwin (1950) and Songs in a Mellow Mood (1954). He was also the pianist on the first solo sides by singer Chris Connor on her album Chris (1954).

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Williams
about_quotes.evidence: Ellis Larkin and I went over and did a tour over there of different bases in Germany.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Williams interviewed by Leslie Gourse, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: that night I met the pianist. His name was Ellis Larkins... With our trio of piano, bass and trumpet, we took care of the dancing.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: During this time in the '40s that I made that album with I thought was a terrific album, the one with Ellis Larkins and the trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Ellis and I became friends and we became such friends that I could play anything... And we had a good time.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: some of the best things that I feel that I made were the next group of things I made with Ellis Larkin.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I went to South America with Earl Hines and Teddy Wilson and Ellis Larkins . . . Ellis Larkin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
